engine.ladder_reasoning: Visitor is checking a physical salon: browsing style-gallery photos, scanning a treatments price list, and confirming location/map is the decision. It is a place you visit, not a defined expertise being sold (trust) nor a portfolio-as-product (work).

goal.one_action: book an appointment at the salon

structure.archetype_note: Classic multi-page local-salon site: home hub + about/team, priced treatments menu, retail product catalog, photo gallery, contact/map. All conversion (booking) funnels off-site to Treatwell/Optios.

mapped.observed_as: What our Clients Say About Us
Treat yourself / Haarlem's Top Hairdresser (BOOK AN APPOINTMENT)
Meet The team / Our Stylists / Our Assistant
WASH CUT & BLOWDRY / COLOURING / TREATMENTS price lists

unmapped.observed: Products page: full retail catalog grid (~14 named product cards, discount tiers) but explicitly in-salon pickup only, no prices/cart
unmapped.why_no_fit: A browse-catalog-without-purchase retail block on an otherwise conversion-single site; not features/pricing/gallery — it is a product-showcase-only catalog that drives no on-page action.

unmapped.observed: Careers submenu under About with per-role job listings (Junior/Mid/Senior stylist, Chair for Rent)
unmapped.why_no_fit: Recruitment/hiring content and chair-rental leasing are a second audience (job seekers / renters), not the visitor-books-appointment conversion; no canonical section covers hiring.

proposed_new.name: external-booking-redirect
proposed_new.evidence: Every booking CTA (Book now, Book Online Now, per-category Book) leaves the site to Treatwell.nl or booking.optios.net; there is no on-site form/calendar. Distinct from generic 'store-badges' redirect — it is an appointment engine handoff.

confidence.why: fetch_quality is full, all 6 pages server-rendered with sections, CTAs, prices, and proof captured; classification is unambiguous (local salon = place).

gap_findings: Booking is entirely delegated to external engines (Treatwell/Optios) — taxonomy 'redirect' mechanism fits but there is no capability expressing an appointment-booking handoff (proposed external-booking-redirect).
Retail catalog with discount tiers but no e-commerce (in-salon pickup only) has no clean canonical section — sits between features/pricing/catalog.
Careers/chair-rental sub-tree is a second, non-conversion audience the single-action model does not express.
Unstructured bilingual EN/NL (mixed testimonials/bios + a hidden /nl/ page) — 'bilingual' capability captures intent but not the ad-hoc, partial nature.
